title: commit/push: use an everything-allowed policy
body: Ignore the global signature policy, which is used to control whether or not we can trust an image, when we're attempting to commit an image from a container, or pushing an image.  The global policy still affects pulling of base images at the time when we pull them, which I _think_ is correct.

comments.body:
The changes LGTM
I'm just a little leery about the security implications and would like @rhatdan, @vrothberg or @mtrmac  to weigh in.
